page: Microsoft Azure E32a_v4 is an EAv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical AMD EPYC 7763 cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of an 819 GB drive, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPUs included.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores align with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, including read-only small block (2075.04 GB/sec) and large block (112.96 GB/sec), also register in the average tier. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in Geekbench image processing and text generation for small LLM models (135M), both scoring in the top 25%. However, prompt processing for larger 70B LLM models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32a_v4 offers a dense memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier single-thread performance. Given the 256 GB RAM capacity, 819 GB of local storage, and average database operations performance (10,153.8 operations on PassMark), this server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded software compilation, and moderate-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E32a_v4 is an EAv4-family virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU memory-optimized ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V, it includes 819 GB of local storage but lacks dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core stress-ng results are in the bottom 10%, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average tier. The instance excels in specific areas, such as image processing and text generation for small language models, which place in the top 25%. Given its high memory density and average multi-threaded capabilities, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database hosting, Redis caching, and multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32a_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32a_v4 features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and local storage for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
819 GB of local storage included without bundled GPUs
Strong performance in image processing and small-model LLM text generation
Suited for memory-bound applications, databases, and Redis caching
